en.extract:
The Raja Ampat pitohui (Pitohui cerviniventris), or Waigeo pitohui, is a species of pitohui in the family Oriolidae found on the western Papuan Islands of New Guinea. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It is also one of the few known poisonous birds.


Taxonomy and systematics
This species was split from the northern variable pitohui in 2013.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized: 

P. c. pallidus - van Oort, 1907: Found on Sagewin and Batanta Islands (western Papuan Islands)
P. c. cerviniventris - (Gray, GR, 1862): Found on Waigeo and Gemien Islands (western Papuan Islands)


References

The '''Raja Ampat pitohui''' ('''''Pitohui cerviniventris'''''), or '''Waigeo pitohui''', is a species of [[pitohui]] in the family [[Oriolidae]] found on the [[Raja Ampat Islands|western Papuan Islands]] of [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It is also one of the few known poisonous birds.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
This species was split from the [[northern variable pitohui]] in 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archive-3-1-to-3-5/species-3-1-3-5/|title=Species 3.1-3.5 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-01-30}}</ref>

===Subspecies===
Two [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 7.1|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.7.1|doi-access=free}}</ref> 
* ''P. c. pallidus'' - <small>[[Eduard Daniël van Oort|van Oort]], 1907</small>: Found on [[Sagewin Island|Sagewin]] and [[Batanta]] Islands (western Papuan Islands)
* ''P. c. cerviniventris'' - <small>([[George Robert Gray|Gray, GR]], 1862)</small>: Found on [[Waigeo]] and [[Gam Island|Gemien]] Islands (western Papuan Islands)
